page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 524 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and cryptographic performance. The server achieves top 25% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core metrics also rank in the top tier. However, single-core execution and memory bandwidth for large blocks fall into the average category. For example, Geekbench single-core scores and large-block memory read/write speeds represent middle-tier performance, indicating a tradeoff where parallelized workloads are significantly more efficient than single-threaded tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64as_v4 offers high resource density with its 64 dedicated vCPUs, making it a viable option for highly parallelized workloads without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The server is applicable to multi-threaded applications, image processing, ray tracing, and moderate LLM inference tasks, particularly text generation using small to medium models. It also supports general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and database operations that benefit from a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance includes 524 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU encryption scores ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, single-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This hardware profile fits parallelized workloads, including multi-threaded decompression, image processing, ray tracing, and moderate LLM inference. It offers a cost-effective, high-density resource allocation for enterprise applications requiring balanced compute and memory.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and local storage for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.

256 GB of system memory and 524 GB of local storage
Strong multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks
